Module com.example.escriturarapida
Package com.example.escriturarapida.controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
com.example.escriturarapida.controller.GameController
public class GameController extends Object
Main controller for the "Escritura RÃ¡pida" game screen.

Manages all the logic of the game-view.fxml view: the timer, word generation, answer validation, level progression, and the transition to the results screen when the game ends.

See Also:
  • GameStage
  • Time
  • Level
  • Words

  • Constructor Details

    • GameController

      public GameController()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Initialization method called automatically by JavaFX when loading the FXML.

      Instantiates the models, hides initial labels, starts the total time counter, loads the level timer and the first word, and registers the listener to validate with the ENTER key.

    • onHandleValidate

      public void onHandleValidate(javafx.event.ActionEvent event)
      Handles the "Validate" button event. Delegates the validation logic to the validateInput() method.
      Parameters:
      event - The action event triggered by the button

    • onHandleEnter

      public void onHandleEnter(javafx.scene.input.KeyEvent event)
      Handles the key press event in the text field. Delegates the validation logic to the validateInput() method.
      Parameters:
      event - The keyboard event captured in the TextField.

    • loadRandomWord

      public void loadRandomWord()
      Loads a random word into wordLabel according to the current level. Uses Words.choiceArrayByLevel(int) to select the correct list and Words.generateWord(java.util.ArrayList) to choose a word at random.

    • loadLevel

      public void loadLevel()
      Updates the level label with the current value of Level.getLevel().

    • loadTime

      public void loadTime()
      Restarts and starts the timer for the current level.

      Stops any previous timer (main and flickering), resets the seconds according to Level.getSecondsForLevel(), updates the time label and starts the new countdown. When it reaches 0, validateInput() is called automatically.

    • validateInput

      public void validateInput()
      Validates the word entered by the player in wordTextField.

      Possible scenarios:

      • Correct: removes the used word, increments the level, shows positive feedback and loads the next level. If all words run out, ends the game with victory.
      • Time ran out: closes the screen and shows the results screen with defeat.
      • Incorrect: shows error feedback and clears the field.

    • showSuccesMessage

      public void showSuccesMessage()
      Shows a success message in FeedBackLabel with green text.

    • showErrorMessage

      public void showErrorMessage()
      Shows an error message in FeedBackLabel with red text.

    • showLessTwoSeconds

      public void showLessTwoSeconds(int level, int seconds)
      Shows wordTwoLabel when the level is a multiple of 5 and the remaining time is greater than 2 seconds.

      Used as a motivational or warning message every 5 levels

      Parameters:
      level - The current game level.
      seconds - The remaining seconds in the current level.

    • changeVisibleMessage

      public void changeVisibleMessage()
      Hides FeedBackLabel and wordTwoLabel after 1 second using a single-frame Timeline.

    • changeColorTime

      public void changeColorTime()
      Starts the flickering effect of the timer when time is critical.

      Calls Time.switchColor(Runnable, Runnable) with two styles: red for the active tick and white for the inactive tick. The initial style is set to white before starting the flickering.
